behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-client handoff: prompt/request builder must pass full taxonomy to the API client. A unit test of a helper can miss wiring/serialization bugs. Rejected System because behavior text is about request content, not real network/OS effects; a stub client can detect omission. Rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model judgment, only payload construction.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with a spy/stub API client, call classify() with a known taxonomy, and assert outgoing request payload contains every allowed label exactly as required.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at boundary between API response adapter and taxonomy guard in module flow. A pure unit test of normalization logic may miss where raw API output is forwarded without validation. Rejected System because invalid-label handling is deterministic and reproducible with stubbed API responses. Rejected Agentic because behavior text targets post-response validation logic, not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run classifier with stub API client returning an out-of-taxonomy label; assert public classify() result is "unknown" and no raw invalid label leaks.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly one-function determinism on normalization of identical input; no module interaction is required to expose failure. Rejected Integration because wording isolates "normalization step" and "identical raw API responses," which disqualifies cross-module dependency. Rejected higher levels since no real infra or model variability is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert exact same normalized label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live calls and consistency conditioned on provider response changes, which depends on real model outputs and their variability. Mocked responses would hide this failure mode. Rejected Workflow because this is a single agentic decision repeated, not a multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected System-only because model decision variability (not just HTTP transport) is central in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated live classifications for the same input; capture both raw provider outputs and final labels; assert label changes only when provider output content changes, and flag unexplained drift.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at interaction between token estimator/policy logic and dispatch gate; test should verify no over-budget request is sent. Unit tests of tokenizer or policy alone can miss handoff bugs. Rejected System because behavior says "before dispatch" and can be validated with in-process stub client observing whether dispatch occurred. Rejected Agentic because this is budget-control infrastructure, not model output correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Configure tight token budget, run classify() with oversized document, and assert module applies policy (truncate/reject) and only dispatches compliant requests (or blocks dispatch per policy).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires real external API behavior and provider token accounting metadata; in-process fakes can always claim compliant usage and hide real failures from tokenizer/prompt mismatch. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about usage metering (infrastructure outcome), not classification quality or model decision correctness. Rejected Integration because exact failure depends on real provider reporting over real API calls.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live API calls with representative documents and production prompt format; assert provider-reported token usage field is <= configured budget, across repeated runs and model versions under test.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration across modules (error classifier, backoff policy, retry executor). This is reproducible with stub API responses and controllable clock/timer. Rejected System because real network/rate-limit infrastructure is not required to validate wait-and-retry control flow. Rejected Agentic because behavior is transport/error handling, not model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub API client that returns rate-limit error then success; verify module delays per backoff policy, retries, and finally returns successful label instead of surfacing initial error.
